title: 2 Under-the-Radar Dividend Kings to Buy in Bunches
author: James Halley
description: Northwest Natural Holding and The Marzetti Company are two underrated Dividend Kings with 70 and 63 consecutive years of dividend increases respectively. Northwest Natural has diversified beyond natural gas through acquisitions and recently hit a 52-week high with strong EPS growth projections. Marzetti, a debt-free specialty food company, has declined 14% this year but shows solid revenue and EPS growth, making both attractive for income-oriented investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company has 70 consecutive years of dividend increases, recently hit 52-week high, secured favorable rate increases in Washington and Oregon, reported 36.4% EPS growth in 2025, and projects 10.1% EPS growth for 2026 with strong dividend yield of 3.59%.

Despite 14% year-to-date decline, company has 63 consecutive years of dividend increases, maintains zero debt providing competitive advantage, reported 13.5% EPS growth and 3.6% revenue growth, recently acquired Bachan's for $400 million, and offers attractive 2.75% dividend yield at depressed valuations.
Mentioned as a larger competitor with shorter dividend increase history compared to Northwest Natural, used as a comparison point but not recommended as a primary investment focus.

title: Got $1,000? Here Are the Smartest Dividend Stocks to Start With.
author: Matthew Benjamin
description: With market valuations high and potential corrections likely, the article recommends eight defensive dividend stocks across consumer staples, healthcare, and utility sectors that have consistently increased dividends for over 50 years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Global beverage leader with 63-year dividend increase streak and 2.9% dividend yield
Consumer staples brand with essential products and 69-year dividend increase streak
Healthcare company with diverse product portfolio and 62-year dividend increase streak
Utility company with 71-year dividend increase streak and essential water services

title: NWN EPS Beats Estimates
author: Jesterai
description: Northwest Natural reported Q2 2025 earnings with revenue of $236.2 million, up 11.6% year-over-year. The company saw positive results from utility acquisitions and rate case settlements, though increased expenses partially offset gains.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed financial performance with revenue growth and positive EPS surprise, but offset by increased operating and interest expenses. Maintained full-year guidance suggests stable outlook.

title: Northwest Natural (NWN) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
author: The Motley Fool
description: Northwest Natural Holding Company reported its Q4 2024 earnings, highlighting strong performance in its natural gas distribution, water, and renewable natural gas businesses. The company initiated 2025 adjusted earnings guidance and outlined plans for continued growth and investment across its diversified portfolio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company reported strong Q4 2024 results, initiated positive 2025 guidance, and outlined plans for continued growth and investment across its diversified portfolio of natural gas, water, and renewable natural gas businesses.

title: Northwest Natural (NWN) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
author: The Motley Fool
description: NW Natural Holdings Company reported its Q3 2024 earnings, with the company's gas and water utility businesses performing well. The company received constructive orders in its recent rate cases, though it was disappointed with a disallowance related to line extension allowances. The company remains focused on executing its capital investment plan and growth opportunities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company reported strong performance in its gas and water utility businesses, received constructive orders in its rate cases, and remains focused on executing its growth plans.
